title: App Router example does not capture $pageview on client-side navigation
body:
## Bug

The **posthog-nextjs** example app (Next.js App Router) only captures a `$pageview` on the initial full page load. When the user navigates client-side via `<Link>` / `router.push`, no `$pageview` is emitted, so analytics severely undercounts pageviews for any SPA navigation.

### Root cause
The App Router does not fire a full navigation event, so a single `posthog.init` at mount only records the first view. There is no `PostHogPageView` component wiring `usePathname()` + `useSearchParams()` to `posthog.capture('$pageview')`, which is the documented PostHog + App Router pattern.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Run the example app
2. Navigate between two routes using `next/link`
3. Observe in PostHog that only the first pageview is captured; subsequent route changes emit nothing

### Expected
Each client-side route change should emit a `$pageview` with the new URL.

### Fix
Add a `PostHogPageView` client component that reads `usePathname()` and `useSearchParams()` and calls `posthog.capture('$pageview', { '$current_url': url })` in a `useEffect` keyed on path/search changes, then render it inside `PostHogProvider` (wrapped in `<Suspense>` because `useSearchParams` requires it). Files to change: `app/providers.tsx` and `app/layout.tsx`.

This is a small, well-scoped fix in the canonical example, so it's worth correcting directly.
